title: Enum caseincomplete#3745
body:
This PR addresses issue #3745. I think, I have fixed the original issue. However, some work still needs to be done for the solution to be complete. The tool should give a runtime warning when the following conditions are met: Case is an enum && (Case is priority || unique) && Not all enums are listed in the case items.  As can be seen in `t/t_case_enum_incomplete_bad.v`, `S3` is not listed as a case item. However the FSM goes into `S3` and will certainly never recover from this state. A runtime warning/error should have been issued here.   
```
[10] cyc==0 in=0 reset=1 state=S0
[20] cyc==1 in=0 reset=1 state=S0
[30] cyc==2 in=0 reset=1 state=S0
[40] cyc==3 in=1 reset=0 state=S0
[50] cyc==4 in=1 reset=0 state=S0
[60] cyc==5 in=1 reset=1 state=S0
[70] cyc==6 in=1 reset=1 state=S1
[80] cyc==7 in=1 reset=1 state=S3
[90] cyc==8 in=1 reset=1 state=S3
[100] cyc==9 in=1 reset=1 state=S3
[110] cyc==10 in=1 reset=1 state=S3
[110] cyc==10 in=1 reset=1 state=S3

%Error: t/t_case_enum_incomplete_bad.v:39: Verilog $stop

```
